title: 00 - Getting real data from ETN (appendix)
text: Install authenticate etn package. Download animal metadata, deployments detections project. Save detections file ready module 01.

title: 00 - Getting real data from ETN (appendix)
code: # run once pak::pak("inbo/etn") install.packages("usethis")

title: 00 - Getting real data from ETN (appendix)
text: Storing credentials .Renviron avoids typing every call (scripts might share). Add lines, save, restart R (Session → Restart R):
code: usethis::edit_r_environ() ETN_USER="your_email@example.com" ETN_PWD="your_password"

title: 00 - Getting real data from ETN (appendix)
code: library(etn) etn_projects <- etn::get_animal_projects() View(etn_projects)

title: 00 - Getting real data from ETN (appendix)
code: id_metadata <- etn::get_animals(animal_project_code = "Inforbiomares")  id_deployments <- etn::get_acoustic_deployments(acoustic_project_code = "Inforbiomares")  all_detections <- etn::get_acoustic_detections(   animal_project_code = "Inforbiomares",   scientific_name     = c("Dasyatis pastinaca", "Raja clavata") ) all_detections <- as.data.frame(all_detections)  # record the download time for reproducibility attr(all_detections, "download.date") <- Sys.time()

title: 00 - Getting real data from ETN (appendix)
text: ➡️ Continue 01 — raw detections mobyData object, pointing file just saved.
code: write.csv2(all_detections, "INFORBIOMARES_detections.csv", row.names = FALSE)

title: 01 - From raw detections to a mobyData object
text: Read generic detection export map columns importDetections(). Attach tag metadata importTags() / assignAnimalIDs(). Understand mobyData data model create one as_moby(). Define id.groups (two species) , reuse everywhere.

title: 01 - From raw detections to a mobyData object
code: library(moby) detections_csv <- system.file("extdata", "rays_detections.csv", package = "moby")

title: 01 - From raw detections to a mobyData object
text: raw file uses source-specific column names (animal_id, timestamp, station_name, …). generic importer maps onto moby’s canonical names. Tip moby also ships dedicated importers common sources — importDetections(source = "vue"), "vdat", "glatos", "otn", "etn" — usually need hand-map columns.
code: raw <- read.csv(detections_csv, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) raw$timestamp <- as.POSIXct(raw$timestamp, tz = "UTC")  detections <- importDetections(   raw,   source = "generic",   col.map = list(     ID          = "animal_id",     datetime    = "timestamp",     station     = "station_name",     lon         = "deploy_longitude",     lat         = "deploy_latitude",     transmitter = "transmitter"      # kept so assignAnimalIDs() can join tag metadata   ) )

title: 01 - From raw detections to a mobyData object
code: tags <- importTags(rays_tags, source = "generic",                    col.map = list(ID = "ID", tagging_date = "tagging_date"))  detections <- assignAnimalIDs(detections, tags)

title: 01 - From raw detections to a mobyData object
text: mobyData data frame, carries metadata (column mapping, tagging dates, id.groups) attribute, don’t repeat arguments every call.
code: # one named list element per species -> drives per-species outputs everywhere id_groups <- split(rays_tags$ID, rays_tags$species)  dataset <- as_moby(   detections,   id.col        = "ID",   datetime.col  = "datetime",   station.col   = "station",   lon.col       = "lon",   lat.col       = "lat",   tagging.dates = setNames(rays_tags$tagging_date, rays_tags$ID),   id.groups     = id_groups )  mobyMeta(dataset)   # inspect the attached metadata

title: 01 - From raw detections to a mobyData object
text: produced mobyData object defined id.groups. bundled rays object exactly , already cleaned — later modules load directly. ➡️ Next: 02 — Quality control, deployment matching & filtering.

title: 02 - Quality control, deployment matching & filtering
text: Import raw receiver-deployment log importDeployments(). Audit deployment metadata checkDeployments() picture plotDeployments(). Match detections deployment windows matchDeployments(). Remove spurious detections filterDetections(). Bin detections regular intervals getTimeBins().

title: 02 - Quality control, deployment matching & filtering
code: library(moby) data(rays_detections) data(rays_tags)

title: 02 - Quality control, deployment matching & filtering
text: Deployment logs arrive vendor/network-specific layouts. importDeployments() harmonises onto moby’s canonical schema (receiver / station / lon / lat / deploy / recover) — pick matching source preset (European Tracking Network export bundled package), supply col.map format doesn’t recognise.
code: dep_file <- system.file("extdata", "rays_deployments.csv", package = "moby")   # raw ETN export deployments <- importDeployments(dep_file, source = "etn") head(deployments)

title: 02 - Quality control, deployment matching & filtering
text: checkDeployments() audits receiver log reports problems — never edits data aborts — returning structured report can inspect act . Supply detections , also cross-checks deployment windows — flagging detections unknown receivers, detections outside window, station-name mismatches: Every check runs default. Use checks = run groups want (coverage-gap check noisiest, since routine servicing gaps legitimate):
code: qc <- checkDeployments(deployments) qc   # flags overlaps, impossible dates/coords, duplicates, coverage gaps checkDeployments(deployments, detections = rays_detections, datetime.col = "datetime") checkDeployments(deployments, checks = c("dates", "overlaps", "coordinates"))

title: 02 - Quality control, deployment matching & filtering
text: plotDeployments() visual companion audit — timeline receiver deployed actively listening. reads log, breaks bar wherever coverage real gap, (invisibly) returns per-receiver coverage table (active days, gaps, coverage fraction). richer log — receivers moved stations, servicing gaps, staggered deployments — group rows coarser unit colour group., e.g. plotDeployments(dep, row.= "station", group.= "array").
code: plotDeployments(deployments)

title: 02 - Quality control, deployment matching & filtering
code: detections <- matchDeployments(rays_detections, deployments,                                station.col = "station")

title: 02 - Quality control, deployment matching & filtering
text: filterDetections() applies sequence opt-quality-control criteria: exact-duplicate removal, detections tagging cut-, per-receiver short-interval false-detection filter (nominal.delay, scaled transmitter delay — standard code-collision filter Pincock 2012 / Simpfendorfer et al. 2015), optional residency/isolation filter (isolation.window, default), unrealistic-speed filter. Removed detections returned data_discarded reason; nothing deleted silently.
code: filtered <- filterDetections(   rays_detections,   id.col        = "ID",   datetime.col  = "datetime",   lon.col       = "lon", lat.col = "lat",   tagging.dates = setNames(rays_tags$tagging_date, rays_tags$ID),   nominal.delay = 120,         # s; enables the short-interval false-detection filter (~30x delay)   max.speed     = 2,           # m/s; great-circle distances (no land.shape needed)   speed.unit    = "m/s" ) filtered                        # a printable <mobyFilter> summary clean <- filtered$data          # the cleaned detections (with a qc_flag column)

title: 02 - Quality control, deployment matching & filtering
text: downstream analyses operate regular time bins rather raw detections. Tip result module equivalent bundled rays checkpoint, later modules load directly can start anywhere.
code: clean$timebin <- getTimeBins(clean$datetime, interval = "1 hour")

title: 02 - Quality control, deployment matching & filtering
text: audited, matched, filtered binned data analysis-ready mobyData. ➡️ Next: 03 — Exploratory analysis.

title: 03 - Exploratory analysis
text: end module able : Load cleaned mobyData checkpoint inspect metadata. Use id.groups generate every output per species automatically. Draw abacus, station-summary chronogram plots. Classify detections diel phase getDielPhase(). Reveal activity rhythms plotPeriodogram() plotScalogram(). Compute residency indices calculateResidency() format summaryTable().

title: 03 - Exploratory analysis
text: id.groups motif id_groups named list animal IDs, one element per species. Passing moby function tells function compute lay results independently group. pass id_groups object almost every function — takes get per-species outputs.
code: library(moby)  # the cleaned, analysis-ready checkpoint (a mobyData object: see ?rays) data(rays)  # the two-species grouping is stored in the object's metadata id_groups <- mobyMeta(rays)$id.groups names(id_groups) #> [1] "Raja clavata"       "Dasyatis pastinaca" str(id_groups) #> List of 2 #>  $ Raja clavata      : chr [1:4] "R01" "R02" "R03" "R04" #>  $ Dasyatis pastinaca: chr [1:4] "D01" "D02" "D03" "D04"

what: 1. A first look: the abacus plot
title: 03 - Exploratory analysis
text: abacus plot shows, every individual, detected study period — quickest way eyeshot data coverage, gaps tag attrition.
code: plotAbacus(rays, id.groups = id_groups) #> Warning: - 'id.col' converted to factor. #>  #> Abacus plot #> ------------------------------------------------------ #>   Individuals:     8 (2 groups) #>   Detections:      1,643 #>   Period:          2023-04-01 to 2023-06-30 (90 d) #>   Points:          cex 1.00 #>   Date axis:       auto -> weekly ("%d %b") #>   Top band:        "%Y" #>   Shading:         seasonal #>   Legend:          1 col #> ------------------------------------------------------

what: 2. Where were animals detected? Station summaries
title: 03 - Exploratory analysis
text: statistic drawn panel axis, counts detections individuals never conflated shared scale. Columns split animals species.
code: plotStationStats(rays, type = c("detections", "individuals"), id.groups = id_groups) #> Warning: - 'id.col' converted to factor. #> Warning: - Converting 'station.col' to factor. #>  #> Station statistics #> ------------------------------------------------------ #>   Statistics:      detections, individuals #>   Aggregate:       station (6 levels) #>   Individuals:     8 #>   Groups:          2 (all -> 3 series) #>   Bar height:      counts (share for avg. detections) #> ------------------------------------------------------

title: 03 - Exploratory analysis
text: Many coastal species shift activity day night. First classify every detection diel phase (simple day/night split based array’s location), visualise hour--date structure chronogram.
code: # representative coordinates of the array (centre of the MPA) mpa_coords <- matrix(c(mean(rays$lon), mean(rays$lat)), nrow = 1)  rays$diel <- getDielPhase(rays$datetime, coords = mpa_coords, phases = 2) table(rays$diel) #>  #>   day night  #>   799   844 # chronograms operate on time-binned detections; bin to the hour first rays$timebin <- getTimeBins(rays$datetime, interval = "60 mins")  # one panel per species (split.by), points coloured by receiver plotChronogram(rays, coords = mpa_coords, split.by = "species",                color.by = "station") #> Warning: - 'id.col' converted to factor. #> Warning: - 'color.by' variable converted to factor. #>  #> Chronogram #> ------------------------------------------------------ #>   Individuals:     8 #>   Detections:      1,643 #>   Period:          2023-04-02 to 2023-06-30 (88 d) #>   Time bin:        60 min #>   Metric:          detections #>   Split by:        species (2 groups) #>   Style:           points #>   Colour:          station (categorical) #>   Diel:            4 lines #>   Date axis:       auto #> ------------------------------------------------------

title: 03 - Exploratory analysis
text: chronogram shows diel structure; plotPeriodogram() plotScalogram() quantify periodicities detection time series. work binned count series, first collapse detections hourly counts per individual (rays$timebin created ). periodogram gives one global spectrum per animal — much power sits candidate period. tidal (~6–12 h) diel (~24 h) bands shaded dominant peak marked.  scalogram (continuous wavelet transform) shows rhythm present — time (x) period (y) heat map. Shown first species:  Note simulated rays strongest periodicities multi-day presence bouts, weaker near-diel (~24 h) component. Real activity data clear circadian rhythm shows sharp 24 h peak persistent diel band running across scalogram.
code: # hourly detection counts per individual: a long ID x time-bin x count table binned <- aggregate(list(detections = rep(1L, nrow(rays))),                     by = list(ID = rays$ID, timebin = rays$timebin), FUN = sum) plotPeriodogram(binned, id.col = "ID", timebin.col = "timebin", id.groups = id_groups) #> Warning: - 'id.col' converted to factor. #>  #> Detection periodogram #> ------------------------------------------------------ #>   Individuals:     8 of 8 (min.days filter) #>   Sampling:        dt = 60 min #>   Method:          FFT periodogram; detrend: none #>   Period range:    2.0-96.0 h #>   Dominant peak:   0 tidal, 1 diel, 7 other #> ------------------------------------------------------ plotScalogram(binned[binned$ID %in% id_groups[[1]], ],               variable = "detections", id.col = "ID", timebin.col = "timebin", ncol = 2) #> Warning: - 'id.col' converted to factor. #>  #> Wavelet scalogram #> ------------------------------------------------------ #>   Individuals:     4 of 4 (min.days filter) #>   Sampling:        dt = 60 min #>   Wavelet:         MORLET; power: log #>   Pre-processing:  gaps: zero; detrend: none #>   Period range:    3-48 h #> ------------------------------------------------------ #>    |                                                                               |                                                                      |   0%   |                                                                               |==================                                                    |  25%   |                                                                               |===================================                                   |  50%   |                                                                               |====================================================                  |  75%   |                                                                               |======================================================================| 100%

text: transition movement two different consecutive residence events (visits) animal’s time-ordered sequence. Visits segmented max.gap (default 48 h): consecutive detections station one visit unless separated longer absence, case later return treated new visit rather one continuous stay. Supplying id.groups builds one network per species. node table reports n_residence (number visits station) mean_residence_h (mean visit duration); edge mean_duration_h mean transit time continuously-observed moves (transits spanned > max.gap absence kept connectivity flagged crossed_gap excluded mean). Tip want discrete visits — one row per arrival/departure — rather network summary, call calculateVisits(rays, spatial.col = "station"). shares max.gap segmentation, counts durations line network’s node table.

title: Introduction to moby
text: moby R package analysis visualization passive acoustic telemetry data, focus marine environments. provides unified set tools spanning data cleaning, temporal spatial classification, movement home-range analysis, residency metrics, spatiotemporal overlap / social-network analysis. functions accept user-defined column names (e.g. id.col, datetime.col, station.col). several functions applied dataset, can wrap data as_moby(): resulting mobyData object carries column mapping (, optionally, coordinate reference system, tagging dates ID groups) metadata, need repeated every call. mobyData object still ordinary data.frame, plain data frames remain fully supported.
code: df <- data.frame(   ID = c("A", "A", "B"),   datetime = as.POSIXct(c("2023-06-01 00:00", "2023-06-01 01:00", "2023-06-01 00:00"),                         tz = "UTC"),   station = c("R1", "R2", "R1") ) md <- as_moby(df) #> Note: the following mapped column(s) are not present in the data and will only matter for functions that use them: timebin, lon, lat. md #> <mobyData> 3 records x 3 columns #>   individuals: 2  (id.col = 'ID') #>   period: 2023-06-01 to 2023-06-01 01:00:00 (tz = UTC) #>   columns: datetime.col='datetime', timebin.col='timebin', station.col='station', lon.col='lon', lat.col='lat'

what: Streamlined Acoustic Telemetry Analyses and Data Visualization
title: Streamlined Acoustic Telemetry Analyses and Data Visualization
text: Streamlined analysis visualisation passive acoustic telemetry data. Acoustic telemetry become one powerful tools studying movement, behaviour ecology aquatic animals. Modern receiver arrays can generate millions detections fish, sharks, rays tagged species — turning raw records robust ecological inference remains slow error-prone. moby provides reproducible, end--end workflow acoustic telemetry data, taking raw receiver exports quality control filtering residency, home ranges, movement, social structure, publication-ready figures. heart package lightweight mobyData object. import detections as_moby(), details column mappings, coordinate reference system, tag transmission delays receiver deployments stored alongside data, describe dataset information reused consistently throughout pipeline. mobyData object simply extends standard data.frame, remains fully compatible familiar R workflows, every moby function also accepts ordinary data frame.

title: Streamlined Acoustic Telemetry Analyses and Data Visualization
text: Consistent data model — declare column roles metadata as_moby() carry whole pipeline. Import & quality control — read detections, tags, receiver deployments (VUE, ETN, OTN, glatos, …); match detections deployment windows; flag false detections (Pincock short-interval / min_lag) metadata inconsistencies. Temporal & spatial classification — time bins, diel phase, season, reproductive periods; centres activity least-cost (-water) step distances. Residency, home range & movement — residency indices visit events; kernel autocorrelated (AKDE) utilisation distributions overlap; rates movement trajectory linearity. Social & movement networks — co-occurrence (association) movement networks, permutation null-model tests, network metrics. Publication-ready visuals — abacus plots, chronograms, actograms, contour plots, receiver-array home-range maps, network figures.

what: Create a moby telemetry dataset — as_moby
title: Create a moby telemetry dataset — as_moby
text: Wraps detection data frame mobyData object: lightweight data.frame subclass carries dataset-level metadata (column mapping, coordinate reference system, tagging dates, ID groups optional land layer) attributes. Downstream moby functions read metadata automatically, column names, EPSG codes tagging dates re-specified every call. mobyData object data.frame, can used anywhere data frame expected (including base subsetting, dplyr, etc.), plain data frames remain fully supported moby functions. Metadata travels object rather living global state, analyses stay reproducible self-describing. Calling as_moby() existing mobyData object updates supplied fields inherits rest, making easy add metadata (e.g. tagging.dates) construction.

title: Create a moby telemetry dataset — as_moby
code: as_moby(   data,   id.col = .mobyDefaults[["id.col"]],   datetime.col = .mobyDefaults[["datetime.col"]],   timebin.col = .mobyDefaults[["timebin.col"]],   station.col = .mobyDefaults[["station.col"]],   lon.col = .mobyDefaults[["lon.col"]],   lat.col = .mobyDefaults[["lat.col"]],   id.groups = NULL,   land.shape = NULL,   epsg.code = NULL,   tagging.dates = NULL,   nominal.delay = NULL )

title: Create a moby telemetry dataset — as_moby
text: data data frame detections (one row per detection, per binned record). id.col Name column containing animal IDs. Defaults "ID". datetime.col Name column containing date-times POSIXct format. Defaults "datetime". timebin.col Name column containing time bins (POSIXct format). Defaults "timebin". station.col Name column containing station/receiver IDs. Defaults "station". lon.col Name column containing longitude (projected x) values. Defaults "lon". lat.col Name column containing latitude (projected y) values. Defaults "lat". id.groups Optional named list grouping IDs (e.g. species, sex life stage), used many functions compute metrics draw plots independently per group. land.shape Optional sf (SpatialPolygons*) object representing landmasses, used spatial functions (e.g. -water distances, UD land clipping). epsg.code Optional integer EPSG code projected (metre-based) coordinate reference system, used projecting coordinates computing distances/areas. tagging.dates Optional POSIXct vector tagging/release dates. Either single value (applied individuals) named vector whose names match animal IDs. nominal.delay Optional transmitter nominal (mean) delay, seconds. Either single value (applied individuals) named numeric vector whose names match animal IDs (arrays mixing tag families, e.g. 60 s 120 s tags). Stored metadata read automatically filterDetections scale short-interval (min_lag) false-detection filter. Usually populated assignAnimalIDs tag table carries delay column.

title: Create a moby telemetry dataset — as_moby
text: mobyData object (data.frame "moby" metadata attribute).

title: Create a moby telemetry dataset — as_moby
code: df <- data.frame(   ID = c("A", "A", "B"),   datetime = as.POSIXct(c("2023-01-01 00:00", "2023-01-01 01:00", "2023-01-01 00:00"),                         tz = "UTC"),   lon = c(-8.1, -8.2, -8.0),   lat = c(37.0, 37.1, 37.0),   station = c("R1", "R2", "R1") ) md <- as_moby(df, tagging.dates = as.POSIXct("2023-01-01", tz = "UTC")) #> Note: the following mapped column(s) are not present in the data and will only matter for functions that use them: timebin. md #> <mobyData> 3 records x 5 columns #>   individuals: 2  (id.col = 'ID') #>   period: 2023-01-01 to 2023-01-01 01:00:00 (tz = UTC) #>   columns: datetime.col='datetime', timebin.col='timebin', station.col='station', lon.col='lon', lat.col='lat' #>   metadata: tagging.dates (1)  # add or update metadata later md <- as_moby(md, id.groups = list(grp1 = c("A", "B"))) #> Note: the following mapped column(s) are not present in the data and will only matter for functions that use them: timebin.

what: Calculate pairwise spatiotemporal associations (co-occurrence network) — calculateAssociations
title: Calculate pairwise spatiotemporal associations (co-occurrence network) — calculateAssociations
text: Builds individual association network, nodes tagged individuals edges quantify strength spatiotemporal co-occurrence. Two individuals considered co-occur whenever detected receiver within time bin. edge weight co-occurrence-based association index — either simple-ratio index (SRI) half-weight index (HWI) — ranging 0% (never co-occur) 100% (always co-occur). Note associations defined operationally spatiotemporal co-occurrence represent shared space--time use, confirmed interaction: co-occurring pair may anywhere centimetres receiver's detection range apart. Results interpreted proxy potential association, statistically tested null model randomizeAssociations. pair compared shared monitoring period (time bins later two release dates earlier two last-detection dates), individual's absence mistaken non-association due transmitter failure death.

title: Calculate pairwise spatiotemporal associations (co-occurrence network) — calculateAssociations
code: calculateAssociations(   data,   id.groups = NULL,   subset = NULL,   group.comparisons = "all",   metric = "simple-ratio",   cores = 1,   verbose = getOption("moby.verbose", TRUE) )

title: Calculate pairwise spatiotemporal associations (co-occurrence network) — calculateAssociations
text: data data frame containing binned detections wide format (time bin x individual matrix, values corresponding receiver/station highest number detections), returned createWideTable. id.groups Optional. list containing ID groups, used calculate stats independently within group, well comparing relationships ids different groups. subset defined, overlaps calculated independently level variable. can either single column name (variable) vector column names, corresponding variables contained data. case multiple columns, interaction used grouping. left NULL, single overlap indices calculated whole monitoring period. group.comparisons Controls type comparisons run, id.groups defined. One "within", "" "". Useful discard comparisons individuals belonging group skip comparisons different groups, required (less computing time). Defaults "". metric One "simple-ratio" "half-weight". cores Number CPU cores use computations. Defaults 1, means parallel computing (single core).  set value greater 1, function use parallel computing speed calculations. verbose Logical; print progress summary console. Defaults getOption("moby.verbose", TRUE). Run parallel::detectCores() check number available cores.

title: Calculate pairwise spatiotemporal associations (co-occurrence network) — calculateAssociations
text: mobyNetwork object type "association": data frame network edges (one row per dyad) carrying node table metadata attributes. edge columns : id1, id2 two individuals forming dyad (network nodes). association co-occurrence-based association index (SRI HWI) pair, percent. co_occurrences Total number time bins individuals detected together. shared_monit_days Number days pair monitored simultaneously. start, end Start end dyad's shared monitoring period. mean_consec_overlap, max_consec_overlap Mean maximum run consecutive co-occurring time bins. subset defined, results stacked subset column; id.groups defined, comparison type column (group1/group2) added. Use networkEdges / networkNodes extract components, plot() draw network.

title: Calculate pairwise spatiotemporal associations (co-occurrence network) — calculateAssociations
text: two metrics calculating association indices : Simple Ratio Association Index: index provides measure proportion time two individuals spend together. calculated ratio number time bins individuals detected together total number time bins least one individuals detected. straightforward metric gives clear indication extent two individuals' space usage overlaps. Half-Weight Index: index modifies simple ratio giving half-weight occurrences one individuals detected. accounts fact sometimes individuals might area one might detected due various reasons. index useful detections perfectly reliable helps mitigate impact missed detections. important acknowledge fish pair detected simultaneously may anywhere centimetres hundreds meters apart, depending maximum detection ranges transmitters. Therefore, caution taken interpreting results, especially making inferences biotic fish-habitat relationships. Useful references: Cairns, S. J., & Schwager, S. J. (1987). comparison association indices. Animal Behaviour, 35(5), 1454-1469. Ginsberg, J. R., & Young, T. P. (1992). Measuring association individuals groups behavioural studies. Animal Behaviour, 44(2), 377-379. Farine, D. R., & Whitehead, H. (2015). Constructing, conducting interpreting animal social network analysis. Journal Animal Ecology, 84(5), 1144-1163. Hoppitt, W. J., & Farine, D. R. (2018). Association indices quantifying social relationships: deal missing observations individuals groups. Animal Behaviour, 136, 227-238.

Calculate residency indices — calculateResidency
text: Computes individual residency indices temporal building blocks derived , returning tidy, fully numeric table (one row per animal) suitable plotting downstream statistical analysis. numeric core used internally summaryTable (formats values publication); use calculateResidency() directly need raw values rather formatted table. Three indices, widely used acoustic telemetry studies (Kraft et al. 2023; Appert et al. 2023), available: IR1 = Dd / Di, proportion days detected detection span (days liberty); maximum residency, considering period animal known alive tag operational. IR2 = Dd / Dt, proportion days detected full study interval (release last data download tag expiration); minimum residency. IWR = (Dd / Dt) * (Di / Dt), IR2 index weighted ratio detection interval study interval. IR2/IR1, ratio IR2 IR1 (gap last detection end monitoring). Dd = number days detections, Di = detection span (days liberty, first/release last detection, inclusive), Dt = study interval (release monitoring end).

Cut-date(s) beyond detections discarded (tag expiry, last download, etc.). single POSIXct applied IDs, named POSIXct vector keyed id.col. remove.duplicates Logical; drop exact-duplicate records (animal ID, timestamp station) filter. Defaults TRUE. nominal.delay Transmitter nominal (mean) delay, seconds, used scale min_lag false-detection window. single value applied individuals, named numeric vector keyed id.col (mixed tag families). Read mobyData metadata (nominal.delay) supplied. NULL (metadata) disables false-detection filter. min.lag.factor Multiplier defining min_lag threshold min.lag.factor * nominal.delay (seconds). Defaults 30 (Pincock 2012 rule thumb). Use plotMinLag() check whether default suits given dataset. min.lag.threshold Optional. Set min_lag threshold directly (seconds), overriding min.lag.factor * nominal.delay. isolation.window Optional residency / sporadic-visitor filter (hours): detection removed gap temporal neighbours exceeds window. NULL (default) FALSE disables . coverage tool, false-detection filter (use nominal.delay ). max.speed Maximum plausible swim speed. single value applied individuals, named numeric vector keyed id.col (multi-species / size-specific limits). NULL disables speed filter. speed.unit Units max.speed: either "m/s" "km/h". acoustic.range Assumed detection range receivers (metres), used speed filter's minimum-distance correction. Note makes speed filter blind consecutive detections closer 2 * acoustic.range. Defaults 600. min.corroboration Integer; speed filter, flagged spatial outlier auto-removed cluster fewer many detections. corroborated group min.corroboration flagged review instead deleted (prevents cascading deletion real relocations). Defaults 2 (remove isolated singletons ). max.iterations Maximum passes speed-filter convergence loop. Defaults 20; use Inf unlimited. min.detections Discard individuals fewer many surviving detections. 0 = . min.days Discard individuals detected fewer many distinct days. 0 = . verbose Logical; print progress filtering summary. Defaults getOption("moby.verbose", TRUE). ... arguments passed calculateStepDistances (e.g. grid.resolution, mov.directions, cores).

title: Filter and quality-control acoustic detections — filterDetections
text: object class mobyFilter (list, print method) containing: data filtered detections mobyData, added qc_flag column ("valid", "overspeed_review" speed-flagged detections retained review); data_discarded removed detections, reason column; summary per-individual data frame raw counts removals filter. filtering parameters used stored "parameters" attribute.

title: Filter and quality-control acoustic detections — filterDetections
text: Filters applied order (filter skipped controlling argument left "" value): Duplicate removal (remove.duplicates): exact-duplicate records (animal, timestamp station) dropped front slip past filters. Pre-tagging: detections animal's tagging.date. Cut-: detections optional cutoff.date. False-detection (min_lag): standard short-interval false-detection filter (Pincock 2012; Simpfendorfer et al. 2015). detection computes shortest interval nearest detection tag receiver; detection whose min_lag exceeds min.lag.factor * nominal.delay (default 30x transmitter nominal delay), -receiver companion , treated spurious (code-collision) decode. Requires nominal.delay; otherwise. Isolation (isolation.window): optional residency / sporadic-visitor tool removes detection time gap temporal neighbours (across whole array) exceeds window. false-detection filter default. Speed: flags steps exceeding max.speed using minimum inter-detection distance (straight/least-cost distance - 2 * acoustic.range, conservative lower bound). flagged step local +/-3-detection window hierarchically clustered; isolated spatial outlier (singleton cluster) removed, whereas corroborated group min.corroboration detections FLAGGED (qc_flag = "overspeed_review") retained, genuine relocation systematic error (bad coordinates, clock drift, -estimated range) surfaced review rather cascaded away. Requires max.speed; otherwise. Minimum detections / minimum days: drop individuals surviving detections days detections. land.shape supplied, distances (speed filter) shortest -water (least-cost) distances rather straight lines. nominal.delay max.speed may single value applied individuals named numeric vector keyed id.col (mixed tag families multi-species datasets); nominal.delay additionally read mobyData metadata supplied.

title: Filter and quality-control acoustic detections — filterDetections

(2015) Ghosts data: false detections VEMCO pulse position modulation acoustic telemetry monitoring equipment. Animal Biotelemetry, 3:55. Kessel, S.T., Cooke, S.J., Heupel, M.R., Hussey, N.E., Simpfendorfer, C.., Vagle, S. & Fisk, .T. (2014) review detection range testing aquatic passive acoustic telemetry studies. Reviews Fish Biology Fisheries, 24:199-218.

title: Assign time bins — getTimeBins
text: function assigns time bins dataset based specified time interval, enabling grouping detection data fixed time periods. Time binning particularly valuable telemetry studies, detection events often aggregated regular intervals (e.g., hourly every 30 minutes). function determines time bins rounding datetime values nearest specified interval using one three methods: "floor" (round ), "ceiling" (round ), "round" (round nearest). essentially serves wrapper round_date(), floor_date(), ceiling_date() functions lubridate package (Grolemund & Wickham, 2011).

title: Assign time bins — getTimeBins
text: datetimes POSIXct vector containing datetimes. interval character string specifying time unit multiple unit round . Valid base units "second", "minute", "hour", "day", "week", "month", "bimonth", "quarter", "season", "halfyear", "year". Arbitrary unique English abbreviations lubridate::period() constructor also allowed. Defaults "30 mins". rounding.method method assigning time bins. Options "floor" (default), "ceiling", "round".

what: moby: Streamlined Acoustic Telemetry Analyses and Data Visualization — moby-package
title: moby: Streamlined Acoustic Telemetry Analyses and Data Visualization — moby-package
text: moby provides comprehensive suite functions methods tailored processing, visualizing, interpreting passive acoustic telemetry data, primary focus marine environments. offers tools spanning data cleaning, temporal spatial classification, movement home-range analysis, residency metrics, spatiotemporal overlap social-network analysis, wide range publication-ready plots. functions accept user-defined column names (e.g. id.col, datetime.col, station.col). avoid repeating every call, wrap detection data as_moby: resulting mobyData object carries column mapping, coordinate reference system, tagging dates ID groups metadata, downstream functions read automatically. Plain data frames remain fully supported.

title: moby: Streamlined Acoustic Telemetry Analyses and Data Visualization — moby-package
text: See help(package = "moby") full list functions, package vignette (browseVignettes("moby")) introductory workflow.

title: Create movement stats table — movementTable
text: Creates publication-ready table per-animal movement metrics (total distance travelled, rate movement, linearity index home-range areas), summary mean +/- SE row. formatter: underlying numeric values computed calculateROM (total distance rate movement) calculateLinearityIndex (movement directness); use functions directly need raw values rather formatted table.

what: Compute node- and network-level metrics for a moby network — networkMetrics
title: Compute node- and network-level metrics for a moby network — networkMetrics
text: Computes consistent set graph-theoretic metrics either association (individual co-occurrence) network movement (location-transition) network produced calculateAssociations / calculateTransitions. Metrics computed per group/subset network carries one. Edge weights interpreted connection strengths (association index association networks; number movements movement networks). shortest-path metrics (betweenness) weights internally inverted, stronger ties act shorter distances high-betweenness nodes identify brokers / movement corridors.

title: Compute node- and network-level metrics for a moby network — networkMetrics
code: networkMetrics(network, weight = NULL, community = TRUE)

title: Compute node- and network-level metrics for a moby network — networkMetrics
text: network mobyNetwork object (type "association" "movement"). weight Optional name edge column use weight. Defaults "association" (association networks) "n_movements" (movement networks). community Logical; run weighted community detection (walktrap) report modularity community membership. Defaults TRUE.

title: Compute node- and network-level metrics for a moby network — networkMetrics
text: object class mobyNetworkMetrics: list nodes data frame per-node metrics (one row per node group/subset). association networks: degree, strength, betweenness, eigenvector, clustering, community. movement networks: in_degree, out_degree, in_strength, out_strength, betweenness, community. network data frame network-level metrics per group/subset (n_nodes, n_edges, density, mean_strength, modularity, n_communities, plus transitivity association networks reciprocity movement networks).

title: Abacus plot — plotAbacus
text: Produces abacus plot: timeline colour-coded detections tagged individual monitoring period. Animals can grouped (e.g. species, sex age) via id.groups, detections can coloured variable (e.g. receiver/site) color., per-animal release estimated tag-expiry dates marked dedicated symbols. Optional background shading (annual seasons custom periods) labelled top time-band provide temporal context.

title: Map the receiver array — plotArray
text: Draws spatial configuration receiver array deployment log: one point per station, optionally coastline /background raster (e.g. bathymetry), metric scale bar. pre-analysis quality-control tool - use inspect array layout, spot implausible receiver coordinates, judge spacing overall coverage running downstream analysis. Semi-transparent circles user-defined radius (detection.range) can drawn around receiver give quick visual sense nominal coverage potential detection gaps, array's convex hull can outlined footprint. text: Statistical testing. Comparisons per-individual metric across time-frames repeated-measures design (animals measured level), default tests respect within-individual pairing: Wilcoxon signed-rank 2 levels Friedman test >2 levels (individuals present compared levels; Skillings-Mack used incomplete blocks package installed). Independent-groups tests (Mann-Whitney / Kruskal-Wallis) used paired = FALSE. effect size (rank-biserial signed-rank, Kendall's W Friedman) always computed, p-values corrected across metric panels (p.adjust.method). Tests suppressed min.n complete pairs/blocks. annotation descriptive/exploratory aid, confirmatory inference: computed complete-case subset, telemetry non-detection often informative (animal absent night left array), comparison can biased ways -plot test corrects. confirmatory inference, fit mixed-effects model (individual random effect; distribution suited metric) check diagnostics. Significance flagging therefore default; full test results returned invisibly. metrics must already columns data; compute upstream (e.g. calculate* family) function stays pure visualiser. Multiple rows per individual x level aggregated agg.fun (mean default).

what: Test association-network co-occurrences against a null model — randomizeAssociations
title: Test association-network co-occurrences against a null model — randomizeAssociations
text: function tests null hypothesis temporally independent space use (.e., animal occurs independently ) using Monte Carlo permutation tests. algorithm permutes entries within column, keeping total number detections individual relative occurrence frequencies across receivers unchanged. Permutations can constrained given factors (e.g., diel phase) performed within monitoring period animal (interval release date last detection). Empirical p-values calculated comparing observed spatiotemporal overlaps null distribution. p-values can one-tailed two-tailed based specified alternative hypothesis (continuity correction applied avoid p-values exactly 0 1).

title: Test association-network co-occurrences against a null model — randomizeAssociations
text: data data frame containing binned detections wide format (time bin x individual matrix, values corresponding receiver/station highest number detections), returned createWideTable. overlaps Data frame containing paiwise overlaps, returned calculateAssociations. constraint.Optional. Variable(s) constrain permutations, e.g. account potential diel seasonal trends animal occurrences. supplied, permutations across time bins restricted combination levels variables. iterations Number Monte Carlo iterations (simulated datasets). Defaults 1000. alternative Character string specifying alternative hypothesis. Must one "two.sided", "less", "greater". "two.sided" tests deviation either direction, "less" tests observed value significantly less null distribution, "greater" tests observed value significantly greater null distribution. Defaults "two.sided". conf.level Confidence level test. Defaults 0.95 (95%). p.adjust.method Method used correct per-dyad p-values multiple comparisons, passed p.adjust. every dyad network tested simultaneously, correction recommended control false positives. Defaults "fdr" (Benjamini-Hochberg false discovery rate); use "none" retain raw p-values, method accepted p.adjust (e.g. "bonferroni", "holm"). dyad-level Association label based adjusted p-value, raw (P-value) adjusted (Adjusted p-value) values reported. cores Number CPU cores use computations. Defaults 1, means parallel computing (single core).  set value greater 1, function use parallel computing speed calculations. Run parallel::detectCores() check number available cores. random.seed Optional. Set seed reproducible randomization. See set.seed. verbose Logical; print progress (permutation progress bar). Defaults getOption("moby.verbose", TRUE).

text: list containing: summary summary table overall results. pairwise_results data frame similar returned calculateAssociations function, additional columns indicating mean null distribution, empirical p-values, association type animal dyad. randomized_overlaps numeric matrix simulated pairwise overlaps, one row per animal dyad (row names give dyad, e.g. "-B") one column per iteration. subset variable specified supplied overlaps, instead named list matrices, one per level subset variable. summary table includes following columns: Type: type comparison. column included id.groups identified supplied overlaps. Subset: column (e.g., temporal subset) included subset variable used calculate supplied overlaps. N dyads: number dyads (pairs individuals). Mean interval (d): mean monitoring period (days). Mean overlap (%): mean observed overlap percentage standard deviation. Mean null distr (%): mean overlap percentage null distribution standard deviation. P-value: estimated p-value(s), indicating significance observed overlaps. Association: association type (e.g., positive, negative, non-significant) based p-value. Pairs Non Sig: number pairs non-significant. Pairs > Random: number pairs observed overlap greater null distribution. Pairs < Random: number pairs observed overlap less null distribution.

Aggregate upstream (e.g. calculateCOAs) to control this. #> Tied (ID, time-bin) instances (first value kept): #>                  timebin  ID                ties #> 1898 2023-06-14 11:00:00 D03 ST01 (1) | ST06 (1) #> 2163 2023-04-24 05:00:00 D04 ST01 (4) | ST05 (4) #> 5302 2023-06-25 16:00:00 R04 ST03 (1) | ST05 (1) assoc <- calculateAssociations(wide) #> Calculating overlap - complete monitoring duration #> Total execution time: 0.03 secs # test the observed co-occurrences against a permutation null model # (iterations kept low here for speed; use the default 1000 in practice) rand <- randomizeAssociations(wide, assoc, iterations = 100, random.seed = 1) #> Total execution time: 0.19 secs rand$summary #>   Type N dyads Mean interval (d) Mean overlap (%) Mean null distr (%) P-value #> 1  All      28                77      0.47 ± 0.81         0.42 ± 0.09   0.515 #>       Association Pairs Non Sig Pairs > Random Pairs < Random #> 1 non-significant            28              0              0 # }

what: Test movement-network transitions against a null model — randomizeTransitions
title: Test movement-network transitions against a null model — randomizeTransitions
text: Tests whether directed transitions movement network (calculateTransitions) occur (less) often expected random movement, using within-individual permutation null model. individual, time-ordered sequence visited locations randomly permuted transition network recomputed; repeating many times builds null distribution edge. Empirical, multiple-comparison-corrected p-values identify transitions (overall network) stronger weaker expected. null permutes order individual visited set locations, preserving set (hence animal's site fidelity) randomising connectivity. therefore tests non-randomness movement order / connectivity, site use . Note movement-network null models active area research; one defensible, transparent choice rather universally agreed standard.

what: Toy acoustic-telemetry dataset (two rays in an MPA) — rays
title: Toy acoustic-telemetry dataset (two rays in an MPA) — rays
text: small, fully synthetic acoustic-telemetry dataset used throughout moby tutorials. mimics two demersal elasmobranchs - thornback ray (Raja clavata) common stingray (Dasyatis pastinaca) - monitored six-receiver array inside small coastal marine protected area (MPA). Coordinates, dates detection patterns simulated (real protected-species locations involved), designed ecologically plausible enough exercise whole moby workflow, including per-species analyses via id.groups. objects come small family tutorial can start appropriate checkpoint without re-running earlier ones: rays_detections - raw, harmonised detections (plain data frame). rays_tags - tag/animal metadata (one row per animal). rays_deployments - receiver-deployment log (one row per station). rays - cleaned, analysis-ready mobyData checkpoint (detections timebin column metadata attached, including two-species id.groups). Raw CSV versions import tutorial also shipped: detections generic hand-mapped layout system.file("extdata", "rays_detections.csv", package = "moby"), receiver-deployment log raw ETN (European Tracking Network) export format system.file("extdata", "rays_deployments.csv", package = "moby") (harmonise importDeployments(..., source = "etn")).

Additionally, function can incorporate additional metadata automatically computes overall means error metrics.

code: summaryTable(   data,   id.metadata = NULL,   id.col = NULL,   datetime.col = NULL,   station.col = NULL,   id.groups = NULL,   tagging.dates = NULL,   tag.durations = NULL,   sensor.cols = NULL,   sensor.titles = NULL,   residency.index = c("IR1", "IR2", "IR2/IR1"),   start.point = "release",   last.monitoring.date = NULL,   residency.by = NULL,   error.stat = "sd" )

title: Generate summary table for tagged animals — summaryTable
text: data data frame containing animal detections. row represent individual detection event, unless 'detections' column included indicate number detections row. id.metadata data frame containing metadata tagged animals, length, sex, transmitter type. columns data frame summarized included final table. multiple rows per animal, variables collapsed merging statistics. id.col Name column containing animal IDs. Defaults "ID". datetime.col Name column containing date-times POSIXct format. Defaults "datetime". station.col Name column containing station/receiver IDs. Defaults "station". id.groups Optional. list element group IDs, used visually aggregating animals belonging class (e.g., different species life stages). supplied, averages calculated independently group. tagging.dates Optional POSIXct vector tagging/release dates. Either single value (applied individuals) named vector whose names match animal IDs. tag.durations Optional. numeric vector containing estimated battery duration deployed tags (days). parameter must either: single numeric value, applied unique animal IDs; named numeric vector, names correspond animal IDs id.col column. multiple tag durations provided, vector must include IDs reordered align levels id.col. sensor.cols Optional. character vector specifying column names data contain additional sensor readings (e.g., depth, temperature). column specified, mean, minimum, maximum values calculated incorporated summary. sensor.titles Optional. Titles sensor readings sensor.cols. provided, defaults column names. residency.index character string specifying type residency index calculate. Options include: "IR1": Residency Index 1, calculated number days animal detected (Dd) divided detection interval (Di), .e., number days release/first detection last detection (days liberty). represents maximum residency value, considering period animal known alive tag operational. "IR2": Residency Index 2, calculated number days animal detected (Dd) divided study interval (Dt), .e., total number days release/first detection last data download tag expiration date. approach provides minimum residency value, assuming animal alive detectable throughout study period. "IWR": Weighted Residency Index, corresponds IR2 index weighted ratio detection interval (Di, number days first last detection) study interval (Dt, total monitoring period). accounts number days detected spread detections within monitoring period, providing measure residency balances frequency detections temporal distribution. "IR2/IR1": ratio IR2 IR1, providing measure gap last detection end monitoring period. choice index can affect interpretation residency patterns, important select one(s) best fits study objectives. information residency estimation methods can found Kraft et al. (2023) Appert et al. (2023) - see references section. start.point character string specifying starting point calculating days liberty. Options include: "release": release day used starting point calculating days liberty. "first.detection": first detection tagging used starting point. Defaults "release". last.monitoring.date Optional. POSIXct object named vector POSIXct objects specifying last timestamp data retrieved, typically corresponding last data download date final day receivers operational. single value provided, applied individuals. named vector provided, names correspond individual IDs, allowing unique timestamps per individual. tag.durations also supplied, total monitoring duration individual estimated based shortest two values: tag expiration date last monitoring day. residency.Optional. Variable used calculate partial residencies (e.g. array habitat). Defaults NULL. error.stat statistic use variability/error calculation, either 'sd' (standard deviation) 'se' (standard error). Defaults 'sd'.

Last detection: date last detection. N Detect: Total number detections animal. N Receiv: Number unique receivers detected animal. Monitoring duration (d): Total duration monitoring days. determined tag duration, provided, alternatively calculated time release last detection dataset (assumed represent final data download). Detection span (d): Number days release/first detection last detection (days liberty) N days detected: Total number days animal detected. Additional columns residency index specified residency.index parameter. residency.specified, additional columns partial residency metrics included. Sensor data metrics: column sensor.cols, mean, minimum, maximum values, using titles specified sensor.titles (provided).

title: moby 1.0.0
text: as_moby() creates mobyData object — data.frame subclass carries dataset’s metadata (column mapping, coordinate reference system, tagging dates, ID groups, land layer) attributes. Downstream functions read metadata automatically, column names, EPSG codes tagging dates need repeated every call. Plain data frames also fully supported, falling back conventional column names (ID, datetime, timebin, station, lon, lat). Helpers: mobyMeta(), is_moby().

title: moby 1.0.0
text: importDetections(), importDeployments() importTags() read harmonise detections, receiver-deployment logs tag metadata common sources (Innovasea/VEMCO VUE, Innovasea VDAT/Fathom, GLATOS, OTN ETN), plus generic mode user-supplied column maps. checkDeployments() audits receiver-deployment metadata (overlapping deployments, missing/invalid dates, implausible coordinates, duplicate records, inconsistent station naming, coverage gaps) cross-checks detections, returning structured report; checks argument selects check groups run. matchDeployments() matches detections deployment windows back-fills coordinates station names; assignAnimalIDs() joins tag metadata assign animal IDs tagging dates. plotArray() maps receiver array deployment log — station positions, nominal detection ranges, deployment status per-station detection effort. filterDetections() removes spurious detections configurable pipeline: exact-duplicate removal, tagging/cut-dates, short-interval (min_lag) false-detection filter (Pincock 2012), corroboration-shielded swim-speed filter (land-aware, -water distances), minimum-detection/day criteria, returning classed mobyFilter object. plotMinLag() helps choose min_lag threshold dataset’s empirical distribution.

title: moby 1.0.0
text: getTimeBins(), getDielPhase(), getSeason(), getReprodPeriod() assign temporal classes; getSunTimes() returns diel boundary times. calculateCOAs() (centres activity); calculateStepDistances() reconstructs movement paths (shortest -water great-circle), returning data dist_m column added trajectories attached attribute (retrieved getTrajectories()). interpolateDistances(), calculateLandDists(), correctPositions(), createWideTable().

title: moby 1.0.0
text: calculateResidency() returns residency indices (IR1, IR2, IWR) building blocks tidy numeric table; summaryTable() formats (metadata, sensors) publication. calculateVisits() extracts discrete residence events (arrival, departure, duration) per animal site, configurable maximum within-visit gap. calculateROM() (total distance, mean/max rate movement) calculateLinearityIndex() (movement directness) return tidy numeric per-animal tables; movementTable() formats (home-range areas) publication. calculateUDs() estimates utilization distributions, defaulting autocorrelated kernel density estimation (AKDE) confidence intervals (via ctmm), classic fixed-bandwidth KDE available; calculateUDOverlap() quantifies pairwise home-range overlap; plotMaps().

title: moby 1.0.0
text: Association networks (individuals): calculateAssociations(), randomizeAssociations(), plotAssociations(), plotAssociationMatrix(). Movement networks (locations): calculateTransitions(), randomizeTransitions(), plotMovements(), transitionsTable(). networkMetrics() computes node- network-level graph metrics (degree/strength, betweenness, eigenvector, community/modularity, reciprocity) , plot() draws either network. Co-occurrence-based association indices (SRI/HWI) interpreted shared space--time use, confirmed interaction; randomisation tests use multiple-comparison correction.

title: moby 1.0.0
text: consistent family base-graphics plotting functions sharing device-stable design: single global cex, colourblind-safe palettes (Okabe-Ito categorical, viridis continuous), optional file/width/height/res export, invisible tidy return. Detection & effort time: plotAbacus() (detection abacus), plotDeployments() (receiver operating-period timeline), plotActograms(), plotChronogram(), plotContours(). Per-station & distributions: plotStationStats(), plotGroupSizeDistribution(), plotMetricComparison() (per-individual metrics across grouping levels, design-appropriate repeated-measures tests effect sizes). Rhythms & periodicity: plotPeriodogram() (FFT periodogram / opt-Lomb-Scargle) plotScalogram() (continuous wavelet scalogram). Spatial & network: plotArray() (receiver-array map), plotMaps() (home-range utilisation isopleths), plotMovements(), plotAssociations(), plotAssociationMatrix(); shadeSeasons() adds seasonal background shading.
